title: `podman port --all` does not print port mappings of pod
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

`podman port --all` does not show port bindings of pod

```sh
~ podman pod create --name portissue -p3333 
c7e9f572a4d8dc0ea14b672ff5eae6ae2b679362706f80fd687e2e74bdc04df6
~ podman port --all
~ 
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a Pod with a port mapping

2. Show all mapped ports with `podman pod --all`

`podman pod --all` does not show anything, but `podman inspect` does show the mapping

**Describe the results you received:**
No port mappings returned by `podman port`

**Describe the results you expected:**
The mapping for the port 3333 of c7e9f572a4d8-infra

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.2

Built:        Mon Jun 28 22:13:34 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @mheon have a theory for this one, could be because infra is where the port assignments are, but infra is not running so `runtime.state.AllContainers` won't return it?
This seems like NOTABUG to me? The description of `port --all` says "all running containers". There are no running containers in the pod. If there were, it would be included in the results?
Good point @mheon, but the port mappings of a pod are assigned to infra so should there be a way with `port` to output those as well?

Why? `port --all` will pick up the infra container. What's more, because of the way port sharing, we will also show that every container in the pod has these ports. Example, for two containers in a port that forwards ports 8080 and 8090:
```
mheon@Marlborough code/podman (main) $ podman port --all
c4103a0819ef	80/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:8080
c4103a0819ef	8090/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:8090
d471f08def0f	80/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:8080
d471f08def0f	8090/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:8090
```

We're showing both containers in the pod as owning every port forwarded. This seems more than sufficient.
Ahhhh okay, I didn't see that the containers in the pod pick up the port assignments like this I just tried `--port all` before adding a container, this looks good to me as is.

@Panke seems like you need to add a container to the pod before `podman port --all` will display anything as infra is a non-running container. Closing this as it is not a bug, feel free to re-open with any questions about why this functions the way it does.
You don't need to add a container - I expect that a `podman pod start` on a fresh pod would also do the job.

> You don't need to add a container - I expect that a podman pod start on a fresh pod would also do the job.
It doesn't. `podman port --all` does not show the port mapping after `podman pod start` even though the infra container is in state `Running` and has `IsRunning` as true. 

It's not an important thing, but violates the POLA principle, IMO. I will just work around it.
Does `podman port --all` show ports for non running containers?
Then I do not see this as a POLA
